_publications._publicationTitle: Sleep Disorders in Pediatric Patients Affected by Neurofibromatosis Type 1: Reports of a Questionnaire and an Apple Watch Sleep Assessment.

_contextSnippet: NF1 patients exhibited a higher resting heart rate during sleep (82 ± 5 bpm) compared to controls (76 ± 4 bpm, p = 0.010). Heart rate variability (HRV) was significantly lower in NF1 patients
details: Detected cardiovascular differences during sleep: higher resting heart rate in NF1 patients (82±5 bpm vs 76±4 bpm, p=0.010), lower heart rate variability (48±6 vs 60±7, p=0.003), elevated respiratory rate (22±2 vs 18±1.5 breaths/min, p=0.004), lower blood oxygen levels (95±2% vs 98±1%, p=0.002)
